description:
You are a Business Intelligence Analyst at a B2B SaaS company. Using the raw transactional logs in the SAAS_ADMIN schema (saas_admin/SaaS2024@localhost:1521/XEPDB1), transform the event logs into standard SaaS metrics for a year-end financial dashboard.

Identify customers who repeatedly upgrade (new_mrr > 0) and then downgrade to a free tier (new_mrr = 0) within 14 days, occurring at least three times. Create a view named PROMO_ABUSERS_VW with columns customer_id, abuse_start_date, abuse_end_date, and number_of_cycles.

Create a view named COHORT_RETENTION_VW showing retention percentages (0-100, rounded to 2 decimal places) for months 0 through 6. Group customers by their signup month (COHORT_MONTH). A customer is considered retained in Month N if their latest event as of the end of that month shows an active subscription (new_mrr > 0). Required columns: COHORT_MONTH, TOTAL_CUSTOMERS, M0, M1, M2, M3, M4, M5, M6.

Generate a table named MRR_WATERFALL_2023 for each month in 2023. The table must include REPORTING_MONTH, BEGINNING_MRR, NEW_MRR, EXPANSION_MRR, CONTRACTION_MRR, CHURN_MRR, REACTIVATION_MRR, and ENDING_MRR. All metric values must be positive and satisfy the equation: BEGINNING_MRR + NEW_MRR + EXPANSION_MRR - CONTRACTION_MRR - CHURN_MRR + REACTIVATION_MRR = ENDING_MRR. Use the following definitions: New (first ever paid event), Expansion (increase in MRR for active customers), Contraction (decrease in MRR where new_mrr > 0), Churn (lost MRR where new_mrr = 0), and Reactivation (regained MRR from previously churned customers).
